pattern: Compute the base absolute-difference sum, then for each position find the largest reduction achievable by swapping in the value of nums1 nearest to nums2 at that position, via binary search over the sorted nums1.
hints: Start from the total without any replacement, then look for the single swap that helps most.
Replacing position i ideally uses the value in nums1 closest to nums2[i]; sort nums1 to find it quickly.
For each i, binary-search the sorted nums1 for the neighbor closest to nums2[i], track the maximum (old gap minus new gap), and subtract that best gain once, all modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: small array with two optimal swaps
already equal arrays need no change
larger example with a clear best swap
swap helps only at one index

stress.description: random positive arrays of equal length over a bounded range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums1 = [rng.randrange(1, 200) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randrange(1, 200) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums1, nums2]

solutions.python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def minAbsoluteSumDiff(self, nums1, nums2):
        MOD = 10 ** 9 + 7
        n = len(nums1)
        base = sum(abs(nums1[i] - nums2[i]) for i in range(n))
        srt = sorted(nums1)
        best_gain = 0
        for i in range(n):
            old = abs(nums1[i] - nums2[i])
            t = nums2[i]
            p = bisect.bisect_left(srt, t)
            if p < n:
                best_gain = max(best_gain, old - abs(srt[p] - t))
            if p > 0:
                best_gain = max(best_gain, old - abs(srt[p - 1] - t))
        return (base - best_gain) % MOD
